_meta.description: Known alert patterns with defined solutions. Agent classifies an alert as ACTIONABLE only if it matches a pattern here. All others are NON-ACTIONABLE and escalated to human.

steps: Perform HTTP health check on host
If HTTP check fails: check EC2 instance status
If EC2 running but website down: attempt service restart via SSM
If EC2 stopped or unresponsive: restart EC2 instance (tag-gated)
Re-run HTTP health check after restart
If still down after restart: escalate to human
solutions.resolution_message: Website was unreachable. EC2 instance restarted and verified healthy.

steps: Check current memory utilization via Zabbix API
If memory > 95%: restart instance immediately (tag-gated)
If memory between 90-95%: create ticket for human review first
Post-restart: verify memory returns to normal range
If memory remains high after restart: escalate to human
solutions.resolution_message: Memory utilization exceeded 90%. Instance restarted and memory returned to normal.

steps: Verify service is stopped via SSM Run Command
Attempt service restart: Start-Service AwsReplicationVolumeUpdaterService
Verify service status after restart
If service fails to start: escalate to human
solutions.resolution_message: AwsReplicationVolumeUpdaterService was stopped. Service restarted successfully via SSM.
zabbix_triggers: Windows: "AwsReplicationVolumeUpdaterService" (AwsReplicationVolumeUpdaterService) is not running (startup type automatic)

steps: Ping host to check basic network connectivity
If host reachable: attempt Zabbix agent restart via SSM
If host unreachable: restart EC2 instance (tag-gated)
Verify Zabbix agent comes back online after restart
If agent still unavailable: escalate to human
solutions.resolution_message: Zabbix agent was unavailable. Instance restarted and agent connection restored.
zabbix_triggers: Linux: Zabbix agent is not available (or nodata for 30m)

steps: Attempt Zabbix agent restart via SSM Run Command
Verify active checks resume within 5 minutes
If still unavailable: escalate to human
solutions.resolution_message: Zabbix active checks were unavailable. Agent service restarted.
zabbix_triggers: Linux: Active checks are not available

steps: Alert classified as NON-ACTIONABLE — CPU spikes require human investigation
Create ticket for human to identify the offending process
Send email alert with CPU metrics

solutions.pattern: CPU queue length is too high
steps: Alert classified as NON-ACTIONABLE — CPU queue requires human investigation
Create ticket for human review
zabbix_triggers: Windows: CPU queue length is too high (over 3 for 5m)

steps: Alert classified as NON-ACTIONABLE — disk cleanup requires human decision
Create ticket with disk usage details
Escalate to human for cleanup or volume expansion
zabbix_triggers: Linux: disk space is critically low

steps: Alert classified as NON-ACTIONABLE — terminated instances cannot be restarted
Immediate escalation to human required
Create high-priority ticket
Informational alert — host recently rebooted
Log event and send notification only
No action required unless part of a larger incident
zabbix_triggers: Linux: System has just been restarted

steps: Alert classified as NON-ACTIONABLE — high load requires human investigation
Create ticket for human to identify root cause
zabbix_triggers: Linux: Load average is too high (per CPU load over 1.5 for 5m)
